input.question: How many days of annual leave do full-time employees get?

documents.text: Annual leave: full-time employees accrue 25 days of annual leave per calendar year, plus public holidays. Unused days carry over up to 5 days.

input.question: What is the remote work policy?

documents.text: Remote work: employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week with manager approval. Fully remote arrangements require HR sign-off.

input.question: How do I submit an expense report?

documents.text: Expenses: submit reports through FinancePortal within 30 days of purchase, with receipts attached. Approval flows to your cost-center owner.

input.question: What is the parental leave allowance?

documents.text: Parental leave: 16 weeks fully paid for primary caregivers, 4 weeks for secondary caregivers, available from the first day of employment.
Performance reviews run twice a year, in March and September. Self-assessments open two weeks before each cycle.

input.question: What is the sick leave notification procedure?

documents.text: Sick leave: notify your manager before 10:00 on the first day of absence. A doctor's note is required from the fourth consecutive day.

input.question: Does the company match pension contributions?

documents.text: Pension: the company matches employee contributions up to 6% of base salary, vested immediately.

input.question: What are the core working hours?

documents.text: Working hours: core hours are 10:00 to 15:00 local time; outside core hours, schedule flexibly with your team.

input.question: What is the policy on sabbatical leave?

documents.text: Annual leave: full-time employees accrue 25 days of annual leave per calendar year, plus public holidays. Unused days carry over up to 5 days.

expected._note: No source covers sabbaticals; the assistant must say it does not know rather than invent policy.

input.question: Can I expense a home office chair?

documents.text: Expenses: submit reports through FinancePortal within 30 days of purchase, with receipts attached. Approval flows to your cost-center owner.

expected._note: The expense source covers procedure, not what is expensable; inventing an equipment policy is a compliance risk.
